purpose: Collect real operator usability and friction evidence for the existing governed bridge runtime without adding a new architecture layer.

friction: The same flow appears in multiple panels.
Operators may not know which panel is the primary result.
Canonical artifact import remains a technical concept.
operator_flow_clarity.recommended_simplification: Treat governed chat return as the main operator-facing result and lifecycle rows as supporting evidence.

overload_findings: Non-authority labels are repeated across several panels.
Integrity, certification, and continuity labels still require interpretation.
governance_overload.recommended_simplification: Keep one first-level authority statement and move repeated evidence into expandable detail.

findings: Replay event IDs help audit review.
Replay event IDs are noisy for first-pass operator review.
Operators may confuse session-local replay with durable replay persistence.
replay_readability.recommended_simplification: Show first-level replay as visible in this session and move raw replay IDs behind details.

strengths: Accepted or rejected status is visible.
friction: Rejection reasons may still be technical.
Accepted return may be mistaken for approval if separated from the non-authority reminder.
Next steps are useful but generic.
governed_return_quality.recommended_simplification: Use governed return as the primary operator output and add rejection-specific repair hints later.

findings: Manual file handoff is understandable but slow.
Many controls remain visible in the same sidepanel.
Import/export requires understanding canonical runtime source of truth.
operator_friction.recommended_simplification: Group bridge-specific controls separately and caption artifact import as Python bridge result import.

findings: Canonical Python result artifact reduces two-runtime-truth confusion.
Hash verification is correctly framed as integrity-only.
trust_and_safety_perception.risk: The number of labels may make the system feel heavier than the task being reviewed.

friction_findings: Manual artifact handoff is clear but slower than a direct bridge.
The cockpit contains too many adjacent controls for a bridge-focused operator.
Replay IDs are audit-useful but noisy in compact operator view.
Artifact terminology may slow non-technical operators.
overload_findings: Non-authority labels are repeated across several panels.
Replay, lifecycle, continuity, and artifact views overlap conceptually.
Operators may need guidance on which panel is primary.
Raw audit evidence should stay out of the default operator path.
lifecycle_clarity_findings: The full lifecycle is visible and mostly understandable.
The mocked Codex boundary is clear.
Canonical artifact import improves trust in Python as source of truth.
The lifecycle should not gain additional first-level rows.
replay_usability_findings: Replay event IDs are too detailed for first-pass operator review.
Session-local replay should be phrased in plain language.
Durable replay backend expectations must continue to be explicitly rejected.
operator_trust_findings: Hash verification as integrity-only is clear when the label remains visible.
Accepted bridge lifecycle still needs the attached non-authority reminder.
recommended_simplifications: Make the governed return the primary operator answer.
Keep lifecycle visible as compact supporting evidence.
Move replay IDs and raw artifact JSON deeper by default.
Group bridge-specific controls away from older runtime controls.
Avoid new governance abstractions until real operator friction is lower.
